problem_title: Travel Rule failures disrupt compliance workflows
problem_description: Failed or incomplete Travel Rule messages create gaps that slow down exception handling and case remediation. Compliance teams struggle to trace, link and resolve these failures without a unified operational view across providers and data sources.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY links message status, originator and beneficiary data, provider outputs and case workflows into one unified system so compliance teams can manage exceptions with full audit evidence and traceability.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule failures to case resolution
workflow_description: SFTY moves failed Travel Rule messages through exception handling, case creation, review, evidence collection and remediation to support structured compliance workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for Travel Rule compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer that connects Travel Rule data, message status, exceptions, cases and evidence into one operational workflow for VASP compliance teams.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage failed Travel Rule messages?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes failed messages into structured case workflows for review, exception handling, remediation tracking and decision logging within a connected compliance environment.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence for failures?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history, message data and exception handling records to support audit-ready supervisory reviews and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates provider outputs without disruption, supporting routing, status tracking, jurisdictional policy application and exception workflows across multiple Travel Rule vendors.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above Travel Rule providers, organizing their outputs into structured workflows but does not replace the underlying providers or their compliance functions.
final_title: Structure Travel Rule failed message workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects Travel Rule failures, originator data, exceptions and case records into audit-ready remediation workflows.
